skill.name: nextjs-supabase-auth
skill.description: Guides secure Supabase Auth integration with the Next.js App Router, including clients, middleware, callbacks, and protected routes.
skill.summary: Build Supabase Auth flows for Next.js App Router projects.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The duplicate external command findings at SKILL.md line 56 are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, not shell execution. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is also a false positive; no cryptographic algorithm or API is present. No evidence found of prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The two static external command detections point to Markdown inline code around related skill names. This text is documentation, not executable Ruby or shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 56 contains only Markdown inline code in a related-skills sentence. There is no Ruby file, shell command, interpolation, or execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography finding points to the frontmatter description. The line describes Supabase Auth usage and contains no cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata text. I found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom crypto implementation in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Next.js App Router authentication has many server and client boundary details. This skill guides Supabase Auth setup with middleware, callbacks, and safer token handling.
actual_capabilities: Guide @supabase/ssr client setup for server and browser contexts.
Design middleware that refreshes sessions and protects App Router routes.
Outline OAuth callback flow for exchanging codes for sessions.
Recommend Server Actions for sign-in, sign-out, and auth mutations.
Identify unsafe token handling and common App Router auth mistakes.
limitations: Does not generate a complete application by itself.
Requires Supabase project details and environment variables from the user.
Does not replace Supabase or Next.js security documentation review.
Does not test auth flows unless the agent runs project tests.

use_cases.title: Add Login to a New App
use_cases.description: Plan Supabase Auth clients, middleware, and callbacks for a new App Router project.

use_cases.title: Protect Existing Routes
use_cases.description: Review route groups and middleware logic before adding session checks.

use_cases.title: Fix Auth Boundary Issues
use_cases.description: Diagnose mistakes between Server Components, Client Components, and Server Actions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan My Auth Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add Supabase Auth to a Next.js App Router project. Explain the files I need and the session flow.

prompt_templates.title: Create Protected Route Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my App Router route structure and suggest middleware rules for protected pages using Supabase Auth.

prompt_templates.title: Review Callback and Server Actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my Supabase OAuth callback and auth Server Actions for session handling, redirects, and token exposure risks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Complex Auth Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my Next.js and Supabase Auth flow across middleware, components, cookies, and route handlers. List risks and fixes.

output_examples.input: I need login and logout in an App Router project.
output_examples.output: A file-by-file plan for Supabase clients, Server Actions, middleware, callback handling, and redirect behavior.

output_examples.input: My protected dashboard sometimes redirects signed-in users.
output_examples.output: A debugging checklist covering cookie refresh, middleware matcher scope, server client setup, and route protection logic.

output_examples.input: Can you review my token handling approach?
output_examples.output: A risk-focused review that highlights token exposure, manual storage problems, and safer cookie-based session patterns.

best_practices: Use @supabase/ssr for App Router server and browser clients.
Refresh sessions in middleware before checking protected routes.
Keep tokens in secure cookies and avoid manual client storage.
anti_patterns: Using getSession in Server Components as the main trust check.
Managing auth state on the client without a listener.
Copying tokens into local storage or custom cookies manually.

faq.question: Does this skill support the Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill focuses on App Router patterns for middleware, Server Components, Client Components, and Server Actions.

faq.question: Does it require @supabase/ssr?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends @supabase/ssr for current Supabase Auth integration with App Router.

faq.question: Can it help with protected routes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains middleware-based session refresh and route protection patterns.

faq.question: Can it create a full Supabase project?
faq.answer: No. It guides application integration and expects the Supabase project to exist.

faq.question: Does it handle OAuth callbacks?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers callback routes that exchange authorization codes for Supabase sessions.

faq.question: Is it suitable for security review?
faq.answer: It can identify common auth risks, but teams should still run tests and review official documentation.
